name: bluetooth-AudioShare

usage_sections: Shizuku API (Privilege Escalation): We use Shizuku to execute code as an ADB/Shell user. This grants us the privileges needed to access hidden system binders.
Turn ON: Disable Bluetooth A2DP hardware offload (Device will reboot).
Set: Maximum connected Bluetooth audio devices to 5 (or at least 2).
Set: Bluetooth Audio Codec to SBC (Highly recommended for low-end chips).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with yohanes/ble-security-research (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tsarpaul/ble-research (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: AudioShare: Dual Bluetooth System Bypass (Android 12-14).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: AudioShare: Dual Bluetooth System Bypass (Android 12-14).
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `bluetooth-AudioShare` from its upstream repository, then run `bluetooth-AudioShare --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. AudioShare: Dual Bluetooth System Bypass (Android 12-14).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use bluetooth-AudioShare because AudioShare: Dual Bluetooth System Bypass (Android 12-14).
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair bluetooth-AudioShare with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bluetooth-AudioShare --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
